subject: Ecology
title: Mining the Abyss
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mining the Abyss.

hook.text: The deep seafloor is littered with metal-rich nodules that took millions of years to form.

buildup.text: These nodules contain manganese, cobalt, and nickel—metals needed for batteries and tech.

discovery.text: Companies want to vacuum them up, but the mining would destroy slow-growing ecosystems.

twist.text: Deep-sea species reproduce so slowly that a mined area may not recover for centuries.

climax.text: The debate pits green energy demand against the last pristine habitats on the planet.

punchline.text: Solving one crisis shouldn't create another.

quiz.question: Why is deep-sea mining ecologically risky?
